How grounded AI agents build, inspect, and verify real cross-platform .NET apps from a single C# and XAML codebase.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

One .NET Codebase, Six Platforms, and an AI Agent With Eyes and Hands Inside Uno Platform Studio 3.0This article breaks down how Uno Platform Studio 3.0 lets an AI agent build, inspect, and verify real cross-platform .NET applications from a single C# and XAML codebase. I will walk through the layered architecture, the two Model Context Protocol servers that give an agent access to current documentation and to a running app, the runtime visual designer and its round-trip back to source, and the human in control loop that ties everything together.
By the end, you will understand each moving part, how the parts connect, and where to start if you want to try the workflow yourself.
The thesis is simple: fundamentals matter more than ever; AI increases your speed; and the differentiator is whether the agent has real context about how your app is built and can verify what it produced.
Uno Platform’s answer is a stack where the agent can read your live visual tree, ground itself in version-correct docs, drive the running UI, and hand control back to you at every merge.
Reference here.
How we build software: three workflows…
